title: Soteria benchmarks configuration
description: Benchmark suite consumed by scripts/run_benchmarks.py. The script produces a github-action-benchmark customSmallerIsBetter JSON from these entries.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this JSON schema; ignored by the runner.

rust_files.description: Single .rs files. Each is compiled once (untimed), then benchmarked with `soteria-rust exec <path> --no-compile`.

rust_crates.description: Cargo crate roots. Each is compiled once (untimed), then benchmarked with `soteria-rust exec <path> --no-compile`.

c_wpst.description: C files benchmarked with `soteria-c exec` (whole-program symbolic testing).

c_biab.description: C projects benchmarked with `soteria-c` in bi-abduction mode (`gen-summaries` or `capture-db`).

path.description: File or project root. Relative paths are resolved against the repo root.

name.description: Label used in the benchmark report; defaults to `path` when omitted.

args.description: Extra arguments forwarded to the Soteria tool.

no_hyperfine.description: When true, time a single run instead of using hyperfine. Use for benchmarks that are too slow to run 10+ times.

path.description: Project root. Relative paths are resolved against the repo root.

name.description: Label used in the benchmark report; defaults to `path` when omitted.

no_hyperfine.description: When true, time a single run instead of using hyperfine.

mode.description: Bi-abduction mode. `gen-summaries` runs on every .c file under `path`. `capture-db` runs on a compile_commands.json.

compile_commands.description: Path to the compilation database, relative to `path`. Only used when `mode` is `capture-db`. If the file does not exist, the runner generates it with cmake.

cmake_args.description: Extra arguments passed to the cmake invocation that produces `compile_commands`. Only used when `mode` is `capture-db` and the database is missing.
